lede: International solidarity coordinator, rooted in the issues of migration, social justice and activist engagement — between France, Africa and Eastern Europe.
about_summary: After several years between NGOs in France and international missions, I have built a career rooted in the issues of migration, social justice and activist engagement — voluntary sector on exile issues in France, positions of responsibility in Central Africa, North Africa, Madagascar and Ukraine. Long committed to gender issues, my queer journey informs my professional practice.
current_quote: Emergency preparedness, institutional partnerships, inter-NGO coordination.
about: After several years working with NGOs in France and on international missions, particularly on programs related to exile, I have built a career rooted in the issues of migration, social justice and militant commitment.
In France, I was very involved in the voluntary sector on issues of exile (outreach, reception, support, advocacy), and I made migration policy the main focus of my research at university.

Internationally, I have held positions of responsibility in Central Africa, North Africa, Madagascar and Ukraine, on programs combining health, exile, humanitarian logistics and project coordination. I have also carried out missions involving exploration, project development, awareness-raising and advocacy.

Having been involved in gender issues for a long time, my personal experience and my queer background inform my professional practice. I now wish to strengthen this link between gender and exile in my commitments, and to continue my development towards positions combining coordination, political reflection and concrete action.
